entry.title: Re: Analytical list page not working after changing filte...
entry.content:
I am also facing the same issue.

In my scenario, I am using SAP Fiori Elements Analytical Page using CDS with Parameters.

On loading the application and on providing selection filter for the first time, the results are populated both in chart and table properly.

But when I change the selection filter and retrigger the GO action, both the chart and table are frozen with no data being displayed.

Not sure if we can use Analytical page with CDS and parameters?

I dont see much help on SAP blogs for the same. Can anyone help?

entry.title: Fieldglass Rates 2.0 Custom Lookup Question
entry.content: Does anyone have documentation for the "Valid On" Key in the Rate Custom Lookup for Rates 2.0?   It is listed as an option but not defined in the Rates Administration (Rates 2.0) guide.

entry.title: Re: Part 2 - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Data Integration Overview
entry.content:
FYI - You can see the updated SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition Data Integration Architecture Overview in the 1st Blog

The latest integration scenarios and best practices content are also available in the Data Integration SAP Help.

entry.title: Re: ISAGENT_MIN_J5 component is not installed
entry.content: It FINALLY turned green! Seems like clearing the IE cache did the trick! MANY MANY THANKS !!!! You are a lifesaver 🙂

entry.title: Logistics Simplified: Resources, Insights, Workshops, and More for Smooth Adoption
entry.content:
Introducing you to the SAP Digital Logistics Adoption Campaigns!

Our mission is to empower you with the knowledge and tools needed to fully leverage the capabilities of Logistics solutions. This program is designed to help you understand the unique value these solutions bring to your business, guiding you through every step of the adoption journey. You’ll find comprehensive resources, expert insights, customer experience sharing, and practical tips to accelerate implementation, ensuring a smooth Go-Live, and maximizing the benefits for your business!

Our current campaign focus is on S/4HANA Transportation Management and S/4HANA Extended Warehouse Management. Join us on this journey through just a few simple steps!

Step 1: Access the Solution Value Card

A value card is a one-pager with key information on the solution, such as its value proposition, key capabilities, and quick recourses. It also indicates what to expect from the Adoption campaign. 

  • S/4HANA Transportation Management
  • S/4HANA Extended Warehouse Management

Step 2: Participate in Expert Guided Adoption Series

To further support your journey, we have organized workshops that are crafted to provide in-depth training, featuring interactive sessions, real-world scenarios, and expert-led discussions. Whether you're just getting started or looking to deepen your expertise, these sessions will equip you with actionable strategies and best practices to drive successful adoption and consumption. Join us to enhance your skills, connect with peers, and challenge us with your unique questions so we can understand your needs better!

S/4HANA Transportation Management

  • Getting Started with S/4HANA Transportation Management: PDF | Recording
  • June 25, 2024: Baseline Activation and Setups
  • July 18, 2024: Optimizer & Package Building
  • August 20, 2024: Insights & Analytics

S/4HANA Extended Warehouse Management

  • Overview & Value Realization: PDF | Recording
  • Feature Implementation Best Practices: PDF | Recording
  • June 13, 2024: Industry Best Practices: Consumer Products
  • June 27, 2024: Industry Best Practices: Automotive
  • July 11, 2024: Industry Best Practices: Retail
  • August 8, 2024: Business AI Adoption in Logistics

We hope these sessions enhance your understanding and ability to leverage the solution, and we hope to see you in one of our upcoming workshops! If you have any questions, please don't hesitate to email us.

entry.title: Navigating Finance and Quote-to-Cash Solutions: Resources, Insights & Workshops for Smooth Adoption
entry.content:
Introducing you to the SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting Adoption Campaign! 

Our mission is to empower you with the knowledge and tools needed to fully leverage the capabilities of SAP Finance and Quote-to-Cash Solutions. This program is designed to help you understand the unique value these solutions bring to your business, guiding you through every step of the adoption journey. You’ll find comprehensive resources, expert insights, customer experience sharing, and practical tips to accelerate implementation, ensuring a smooth Go-Live, and maximizing the benefits for your business!

Our current campaign focus is on S/4HANA Group Reporting with assets for more products coming soon. Join us on this journey through just a few simple steps!

Step 1: Access the S/4HANA Group Reporting Value Card

A value card is a one-pager with key information on the solution, such as its value proposition, key capabilities, and quick recourses. It also indicates what to expect from the Adoption campaign. 

Step 2: Check out the Expert Guided Adoption Series

To further support your journey, we have organized workshops that are crafted to provide in-depth training, featuring interactive sessions, real-world scenarios, and expert-led discussions. Whether you're just getting started or looking to deepen your expertise, these sessions will equip you with actionable strategies and best practices to drive successful adoption and consumption. Join us to enhance your skills, connect with peers, and challenge us with your unique questions so we can understand your needs better!

  • S/4HANA Group Reporting: An Introduction (Public and Private Cloud): PDF | Recording
  • S/4HANA Group Reporting: A Closer Look (Public and Private Cloud): PDF | Recording
  • How to Adopt Group Reporting in Public Cloud – Accounting （Public Cloud): PDF | Recording

We hope these sessions enhance your understanding and ability to leverage the solution! If you have any questions, please don't hesitate to email us.

entry.title: Re: Successfactor Monthly Time Sheet can't be enabled in...
entry.content: Did you find any solution for this?

entry.title: Re: Item category determination in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi varunvenkat,

Thank you for this blog, very insightful.  May i know if SAP has a documentation that lists standard item categories and how/where it's intended, meaning what's the related business process for such item category?  You used the example "CBLN represents a free of charge item, which means a product with that item category won’t be billed later on", which is good... but how about the rest?  I can see there are hundreds of these categories.  I'm new to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, is there a documentation for each category that will guide me in starting the learning and understanding the functionality of each of these categories?

Regards,

Cathy

entry.title: Req to create 2 sales order using bapi with the data from JSON payload by using SAP odata GW
entry.content:
Hi All,

I need to create multiple sales orders using a BAPI in SAP using sap gateway call.I am getting data in deep entity format(JSON FILE).Kindly suggest me which method i can enhance so that i read the payload data in DPC ext class

entry.title: Re: migrate data from SAP R/3 (4.7) to SAP S/4HANA Cloud...
entry.content:
Hi @KaviM,

There is no direct data transfer from SAP R/3 to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. For the direct migration approach, the lowest supported SAP ERP release is ECC 6.0 (SAP NetWeaver 7.0).

I would recommend exploring the Staging Table approach.

Hope this helps.

Arturo

entry.title: Modernize your Commerce Stack with Composable Content Management
entry.content: Ever wonder how you can build more meaningful and seamless digital commerce experiences? It’s simple, you need reusable content blocks that are flexible, scalable, and incredible. That’s why SAP Commerce Cloud and Contentful have partnered to deliver a solution that helps businesses deliver top of the line customer experiences. Join us to learn how the partnership between SAP Commerce Cloud and Contentful has helped organizations create meaningful customer experiences from comprehensive business insights to individualized shopping experiences. Register for the webinar here.

entry.title: Re: How to upgrade SAP SuccessFactors demo systems to 1H...
entry.content: Update: 31st May. New template NGD 2024v1 released for Partner NCL orders in DC57 and DC70. Refer to the above links for information on how to update your NCL instance, or contact the demo administrator within your partner organization.

We noticed that the payslip view via MS team is not password protected . Is there a way to make it secure by protecting with a password ?

Saima

entry.title: Set up Procedure w classification for PR
entry.content:
SAP Guru's, For PR REL Strategy with classification, it is prompting info error about the long text. 

So, in our SAP system, we're already using a class. Now I created a new class for my region but it is not letting me add Rel Group - Rel. Obj - Class (new). Why?

Rel. group Rel. obj. Overall rel.    Class      Description

011_  FRG_EBAN     Group 01
021_  FRG_EBAN     Group 02
S11X  FRG_GF_EBANGroup S1
S21X  FRG_GF_EBANGroup S2

entry.title: Re: How to rename cell Totals - SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
- hide the total of the "Tonnes" column - value 335.000.

Click on the cell with value 335.000 and from styling menu , change the font to White color

entry.title: IBP to Datasphere with mass extraction of key figure data
entry.content:
Hello Folks!

I'm having problems doing mass extractions from IBP to Datasphere. First i've tried the OData APIs, but According to the API documentation: "If you need to extract large amounts of data, we recommend that you do so using SAP Cloud Integration for data services."

 

After digging for a while, however, I've discovered that the service is no longer available on BTP:

What Is the best practice in this case, then? Extract the data using CI-DS? Or should I just go to Integration Suite with Cloud Integration Like in this blog ?

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi, @David_Chaviano 

I am someone who would like to switch careers mid-life. I have been involved in Finance where SAP is being used and was fascinated with what SAP can do and its impact in the different businesses that use it. I would like to upskill myself in SAP and be able to learn new things and apply it in the next work that I will be a part of.

Thank you for this wonderful opportunity.

entry.title: Re: Restrict to close Production Order If Issue & Receipt...
entry.content: query is working only if production order is in Release mode and issue for production is not completed. query is not restricted to close if receipt of production after issue for production is done

entry.title: Re: Restrict to close Production Order If Issue & Receipt...
entry.content: query is working only if production order is in Release mode and issue for production is not completed. query is not restricted to close if receipt of production after issue for production is done. I want to restrict user to close production order mistakenly any of them is pending stage

entry.title: How to make an Outbound delivery not relevant for POD?
entry.content:
Hi,

Currently, in our sales process, the delivery is relevant for POD. I understand this is because the delivery item category and BP both are marked for "POD Relevant".
Could anyone please help how can I make the delivery not relevant for POD? If I mark the delivery item category and BP not relevant for POD, then during Outbound delivery creation the system throws the error - "Delivery item 000010 of delivery is not relevant for POD".

Request to please provide your inputs to make the process not relevant for POD.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Login to SAP Digital Assistant service at BTP using SAPDAS cli
entry.content: Joule is not specific to success factors. As of now, it is available with success factors. The availability of Joule as a service is planned for 2024 and will be made available soon.

entry.title: Error while saving Business partner
entry.content:
Hi,

When I am creating a business partner in S4 system using tcode BP, and trying to save it the system gives the error - 

"Application object BUPA: Table BUS000FLDS does not exist"

Request to please help in fixing this issue.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Customer with multiple distribution channels
entry.content: In short, a customer who is assigned both the distribution channels .i.e. '03' AND '10' should NOT be displayed in the report while customer who is assigned distribution channels - '02', '07' , '10' should be displayed. In either cases, distribution channel - '10' is common

Nidhi Singh

entry.title: Re: Chart of depreciation: Ledgers of assigned ledger gro...
entry.content: hey sir, have you solved your problem?

entry.title: Re: SAP DM - Order POD only show work when available at w...
entry.content:
Please try the below if it works for you. 

Go to POD Designer to create a copy of order pod. you have selection to default your WC, material etc. Also, you can enable show sfc status filter so that user can select the sfc status and then result can be filtered accordingly. 

This way, a much suited result set can be obtained. Alternatively, you can plan to extend via PPD, UI etc and have your own UI screen to your needs if that fits your requirement better.

entry.title: Re: How to write Data into S4 Odata via CAP
entry.content:
Hello Rewati,

The Database transactions are automatically managed within an OData servicer event, and we don't have to open a new transaction! The CAP community has maintained excellent documentation, you can refer to these:

1. The CRUD actions can be done through the CQL, similar to SQL statements.

2. Adding external service to a CAP project.

3. Transaction Management.

 

I hope this helps!

Harsha

entry.title: Re: Wily Introscope offline no matter what :-(
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

The 404 error in the defaultTrace is a known issue which has been recorded in KBA 2352112 - Enterprise Manager is offline in Infrastructure Preparation due to error 'com.sap.smd.introscope.em.IntroscopeException: http status code 404' - SAP Solution Manager 7.1 and 7.2. 

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

entry.title: Re: FSCM Credit Magt: Manual score update
entry.content:
In Standard SAP you cannot overwrite the score. The reason being that the score is used to derive the Risk Class and the Risk Class can already be defined manually.

However, you could overwrite the score using a workaround. You can for example, define a feld under "Creditwothiness data" and use it to enter the desired score manually.

Then create a new scoring rule that calculates the score as equal to the value you have already entered in the new field manually. 

So you would still need to execute "calculate score" under Credit profile for the BP, but the score will be equal to your manually entered value.

entry.title: Failed to load destination. Caused by: No user token (JWT) has been provided. This is strictly neces
entry.content:
Hi, I am attempting to call an SAP OData service from a CAP (Cloud Application Programming) application using a CDS spawn job. I am utilizing a destination with Principal Propagation but encountering the following error:

logs:-
[cds] - connect to sap_odata > odata-v2 { destination: 'sap_destination', forwardAuthToken: true }
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR [remote] - Error: Error during request to remote service:
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR Failed to load destination.
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR at run (/home/vcap/app/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/remote/utils/client.js:310:31)
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR at process.processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:95:5)
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR at async RemoteService.<anonymous> (/home/vcap/app/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/remote/Service.js:276:20)
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR at async next (/home/vcap/app/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/srv-dispatch.js:76:17)
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR at async RemoteService.handle (/home/vcap/app/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/srv-dispatch.js:74:10)
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR at async Object.methodName(/home/vcap/app/srv/util/myfile.js:102:24)
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR at async mycatfile (/home/vcap/app/srv/util/mycatfile.js:524:22) {
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR statusCode: 502,
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR reason: {
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR message: 'Error during request to remote service: \n' +
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR "Failed to load destination. Caused by: No user token (JWT) has been provided. This is strictly necessary for 'PrincipalPropagation'.",
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR request: {
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR method: 'POST',
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR url: '/xxxx/xxxxxx_SRV/dummyEntitySet',
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR headers: {
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR accept: 'application/json,text/plain',
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR 'accept-language': 'en',
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR 'content-type': 'application/json',
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR 'content-length': 311,
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR authorization: 'Bearer ...'
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR }
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR },
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR correlationId: '507a3d4f-bf6d-4296-4cc1-f6b8f070ee2b'
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR }
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR } 

code :- 

 

const myFn = async (req, data) {
    cds.spawn({ 
          user: cds.context?.user,
          tenant: cds.context?.tenant,
          headers: cds.context?.headers || req?.headers,
          after: iafterTime }, async (req) => {
          sendToSAP(req, customID)
    })
    return 'processing in backgorund'
}

const sendToSAP = async (req, customID) => {   
    const payload = {}  
    const oSAPService = await cds.connect.to("sap_destination")
    let headers = { "Content-Type": "application/json" }
    headers.authorization = cds.context?.headers || req?.headers
      const response = await oSAPService.send({
        method: "POST",
        path: "/xxxx/xxxxxx_SRV/dummyEntitySet",
        data: payload,
        headers: headers
      });
}

 

 

 
"dependencies": {
"@sap-cloud-sdk/http-client": "^3.0.1",
"@sap/cds": "6.8.4",
"@sap/cds-dk": "6.8.3",
},
"engines": {
"node": "^18.0.0"
}
 
this is workign without spawn job.

a service key can be used in ADT to connect to an SAP BTP, ABAP environment system: Connect to the ABAP system

The service key can be created while selecting the corresponding ABAP service instance in SAP BTP Cockpit: Creating a Service Key

Peter

entry.title: Data Action to create values for existing dimension members but yet no values
entry.content:
Hi all,

i am trying to create a Data action which creates a specific value (zero, not Null) for a given time horizont and for the members of each dimension I selected beforehand via prompt.

First approach:

MEMBERSET[d/Dim1]= %Paramater1%

MEMBERSET[d/Dim2]= %Paramater2%

etc...

Data([d/Measures] ="Measure1")=0

also etc...

This DA only writes back zeroes for already existing values.

My desire is to "fill in" the null values with zeroes in this case.

Any idea? TYIA

entry.title: Vector Engines Overview
entry.content:
Well, this is not a question but my general understanding of Vector engine and my personal note of it.

Vector Engine

SAP previously used SAP HANA cloud as the database which was a multi-model database. Now with the release of vector engine (in sap Hana cloud) we can store the data in form of vector embeddings also.

Now also the database is multi-model. We have just added the vector engine to the database.

 

So, SAP already had these types of models like spatial data, graph type data, JSON etc. Now we have a vector embedding type of data also which is managed by the vector engine.

SAP does not only store these data or embeddings but also perform analytical computations with them to leverage fast query and provide contextual data to LLM’s.

 

We have a new data type to store these types of data namely -> REAL_VECTOR.

A constructor -> TO_REAL_VECTOR.

And two new distance calculating functions -> l2distance(), cosine_similarity().

L2Distance() calculates the Euclidean distance between the two vectors.

Cosine_similarity() is based on the angle the two vectors make with each other, More angle means more similarity.

These distance calculations are important as they help to calculate the relation between two vectors and how closely they match with each other.

 

We can directly query the vectors using normal SQL-like syntax from our application layer. Not just query but also perform in memory computations and also use the vectors for Retrieval Augmented Generation(RAG) to get the contextual data from the LLS’s used.

 

What is vector embeddings?

These are Mathematical data representations of any objects in a multidimensional vector space. These vector embeddings are generated by AI which are good with certain data types like maybe pdf or word.

It converts the unstructured data to a format that can be processed quickly.

Now that we have vector embeddings, these can be used to find the matching record using the distance in the vector space. All the related docs are kept at one place so the distance is less.

 

 

 

From this figure we can understand the whole use case.

We have uploaded some pdf files to the cloud database. The DB will create the chunks of texts and convert it into vector embeddings using AI and store it in vector space in the vector engine in HANA DB. Now this data is accessible throughout the DB.

A user comes and queries for the word “DATABASE”, this query is now converted to a vector itself and with the use of semantic search it queries the DB with the vector of our string. We might not have the exact word in our DB. Now this query is processed through the LLM model for giving the answer, as we don’t have database string in our DB. It will look for the closest thing possible logically based on the distance measurement technique. Therefore, fish is not considered but the string ‘TABLE’ is given as output.

Hence we have utilized the vector engine in order to send contextual data regarding our own DB data to the LLM to give accurate results with least hallucinations. This is the concept of Retrieval augmented generation also(RAG) which uses third-party data source apart from the dataset used to train the LLM.

entry.title: Re: Connecting to Cloud Foundry provided by BTP trial
entry.content:
Hello Felix,

before login to CF, please check that platform user you are using to connect to Cloud Foundry is both a member of an org in Cloud Foundry and a space developer member in a CF space.

Please also try to use the SSO Passcode option where you have to open an URL with your platform user and then enter a provided sign-in passcode for login in BAS. 

Best regards,
Peter

entry.title: 集团报表层面-库存抵消的加价比率 / Customer want to know how to run Interunit elimination of profit in inventory
entry.content:
在GR模块，怎么实现库存的抵消？每个物料在公司间交易中都是有价格变动的，怎么实现这个价格变动导致的集团层面的库存抵消？

how to run Interunit elimination of profit in inventory?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: dynamically resize width of tables next to each other?
entry.content: thank you @JefB . I have voted for your impr. request.

entry.title: Re: Upgrade SAP Business Application Studio to SAP Build Code
entry.content:
Hi Dan,

SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) is our offering for professional developers and lets you easily create extensions.

SAP Build is a suite of products containing SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Process Automation, SAP Build Work Zone, and SAP Build Code. 

If you are interested in the value proposition of SAP Build Code, here they are:

  1. It is a holistic offering comprising design-time and run-time capabilities for cloud development – you get all the services needed for cloud development together.
  2. It has a simplified commercial model.
  3. It has Joule support in the design time, allowing you to leverage generative AI for building your applications.
  4. It automatically ensures that you follow the BTP Developer’s Guide and always follow SAP best practices. In the IDE, many guides help you to create and deploy your first cloud applications (Full-Stack, UI)
  5. It lets you manage your projects via the SAP Build Lobby enabling fusion development with the other SAP Build products.

Hope this helps.

entry.title: First SAP Inside Track Event in Auckland
entry.content:
Join the Auckland SAP Inside Track for an insightful discussion on the latest trends and best practices in SAP technology. This event is designed for professionals who are eager to deepen their knowledge of SAP Business Technology Platform.
Network with like-minded individuals, share experiences, and gain valuable insights that can enhance your skills and career opportunities. Whether you are an experienced SAP professional or just starting in the field, this meetup promises to provide valuable takeaways and foster connections within the Auckland SAP BTP community.

Come hear from a couple SAP Champions as well as some local BTP #rockstars!

Agenda:
1. SAPPHIRE updates - BTP Growth and Acceleration!
2. SAP BTP Architecture - Best Lessons Learned
3. SAP Cloud Application Programming (CAP) - Riding the Learning Curve!
4. How to help your organisation be a Future Ready Enterprise (FRE)

RSVP now to secure your spot at this informative event!

There will be drinks and nibbles after the event.
Space is limited so register early!

Register using the below link
https://www.meetup.com/auckland-sap-btp-meetup-group/events/301356488/

Tuesday, June 25, 2024
4:00 PM to 5:30 PM NZST
Location:  Lagom, 16 Anzac Avenue · Auckland

entry.title: how to stop migration cockpit long time running job
entry.content:
when you are progressing the migration cockpit, sometimes the job is blocked for a long time, and it impacts the next job working. so here it needs to trouble shooting the blocked job and pause the job. 

this blog is to share with you how to stop the migration cockpit long time running job.

1. find project UUID in migration cockpit project

Project UUID: LVkQorLa7jwjlTnEwu8IaG

2. find the OBJECT UUID in the migration cockpit project

OBJECT UUID LVkQorLa7jwjlU0}vLdItG

3. find the user ID who blocks the job

4. input above collected value into Table name: /LTB/MC_EVENTLOG

5. get the activity UUID and activity

Find the activity UUID - 993645B344D61EDEB48F939CD8CE6FB3

6. clear or delete the blocked activity.

entry.title: Re: Upgrade SAP Business Application Studio to SAP Build Code
entry.content:
Hi @Masoom,

I hope my answer related to SAP Build Code value proposition helps you.

You are right, the IDE of Build Code is based on SAP Business Application Studio but also has Joule, Guided Development based on BTP Developer's Guide to make your extension creation easier.

However, as I mentioned, SAP Build Code is not just about design time but also about run time capabilities. You should take a holistic view of the product to decide if it's the right fit for you.

entry.title: How to deal with old roles with the same but no restriction after a new role with restriction?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Guru,

I am a functional consultant and for only enabling authorized users to change PM orders, one of the options is to limit planner group (equipment master) + PM Order type.

Authorization Object   I_INGRP  ---- Planner Group

Authorization Object   I_AUART -------Order type

However there are many role with no restrictions to these 2 authorization objects

My question is:

After a new role is created for the proper limitation, how do we deal with the old roles which have no restriction?  The users with those roles are still able to access.

Please kindly help,

Sally

.

Greetings of the Day!

I want to know about the process of Sending data's from Environment management module to SAP Data sphere.

Also Could you please  guide me how the data's should flow from EM to Sustainable control Tower Module.

 

Thanks & Regards

Tam

entry.title: Re: Tell us what guides you'd like to have GitHub enabled
entry.content:
Hi @DominikTylczyn ,
thank you for your input.
We do not have a strong focus on SAP S/4HANA documentation, because we assume that GitHub is not that popular amongst business users. As an example, this administration guide in the S/4HANA area hasn't been getting any feedback since we enabled the guide in 2021.

However, there was a new feature released this year on SAP Help Portal for exactly that target group: Comments. As a built-in feature it's easier to use and also allows engaging directly with the responsible authors. However, there is no dedicated process flow for contributions like in GitHub using pull requests (you could still make proposals in a comment, though). 

This new feature is not enabled everywhere, but if guide owners decide to do it, it's a matter of minutes to do it. There was a high interest in the new feature amongst colleagues writing documentation for SAP S/4HANA, but I cannot predict how soon the guides you're interested in will use that option.

The Open Documentation Initiative was mainly targeted at developers from the very beginning. Developers that already use GitHub then have a comfortable way to report and track documentation issues in the environment they use already anyway. I believe for them the initiative will remain valuable.

Cheers,
Jens

entry.title: Re: Connecting to Cloud Foundry provided by BTP trial
entry.content:
Thanks for you reply, Peter.

I changd the memberships as suggested by you, but nothing changed. Also login using SSO does not work for CF.

Kind regards,

Felix

entry.title: Re: Error while saving Business partner
entry.content:
Hi,

The error "Application Object BUPA: Table BUT000FLDS does not exist" is triggered due to a reference to BUT000FLDS in the transaction despite the table not being available in the data dictionary. To address this problem, check for the presence of an entry for BUT000FLDS in transaction BUSG or table TBZ1H.

Furthermore, Consult with the ABAP team to resolve it.

Regards

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Conversion projects - Tips on Asset Accounting – Preparation Phase
entry.content: Congrats. A very nice and interesting blog with lot of details. 😊

entry.title: impact H4S4 support packages on S4 ERP

Besides data protection & access authorisation the question is what impact the maintenance in the integrated option got on the overall S4 System. Do the monthly support packages for HCM cause any downtimes for the ERP or not? 

Thank you

Getting Error : Address already in use .
Error Screen shot : 

Please help me on this 

Regards ,
Vasanth .

entry.title: Re: Involving software engineers in shaping the future of Clean ABAP and the Style Guide repository
entry.content:
Hi @haeuptle,

Does SAP use Clean ABAP internally?

I have looked through some code on an S/4 HANA 2023 system, and am specifically looking for examples of where SAP developers code without encoding prefixes (Hungarian notation) in their variables, something which is a hot topic in our team.

While it appears there is no real consistency in how SAP developers use encoding prefixes, they still, however, seem to prefer having them, which is not in line with the Clean ABAP recommendations.

While I understand these are only guidelines and not rules, I wonder why SAP does not appear to adhere to that.

Thanks and Regards,

Martin

entry.title: Re: Java Script in SAP Build Apps
entry.content: Thanks for sharing Dan.

entry.title: SAP CAP Java asynchronous event handlers
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We wanting to allow users to trigger a long running job from a UI5 application with the custom action in CAP Java.

Once the user clicks on  a button, we call a custom action which in turn triggers (emit --> event) the LONG running job. But we want the response from the custom action straight away while the trigged job is now running in the background. The UI will refresh and update the stats that are in a DB in a setInterval loop  till all jobs are processed. 

Basically we need to start another and be able to return tot the UI. The emit event is synchronous hence we need some other solution.

Button --> call action in CAP JAVA application --> emit event to start a LONG process but return to UI while job is running backgroup.

I am struggling with "RequestContextRunner" and appreciate any help / guidance with this.

And also which phase i need to add this into so it becomes asynchronous.

@skymu89 

@marcbecker 

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Introducing the Background Processing Framework
entry.content:
Hi @mario_lenz,

The bgPF method save_for_execution( ) must be called in the save sequence - you can call all bgPF methods inside the save sequence.

As alternative you can create the operation and set it into the bgPF process in the interaction phase; Method save_for_execution( ) must then be called on the same bgPF instance as in the interaction phase.

This information was indeed missing in the blog - I will update it accordingly! Thanks for pointing this out.

entry.title: Two questions regarding CDS view association and performance
entry.content:
Hi experts,

 

Question 1:

Assuming I have 2 DB tables, table A and table B, based on which I create 2 views, view A and view B.

A and B has association relationship, let's say header and item.

View C selects from A associates B, it defines fields from both view A and B.

View D selects from View C, but it only defines fields from view A. 

My question is, will table B be associated if I select data from view D?

 

Question 2 :

Will the performance be improved if selecting CDS view by using key fields, in case CDS view key fields are not DB table key fields? Could someone explain more on the usage of key fields of CDS view?

Notes: question 2 has no relationship to question 1.

 

Regards,
Eric

entry.title: In SAP B1, system considering Future Date Changes for all Transaction Documents
entry.content:
Dear Expert,
 
In SAP business One, one of the document is added post dated.
 
We have checked Document setting and already Future Posting Date is un-tick. 
 
How is it possible please guide us on how this is possible.
 
After investigation, we have found that, if entry is created post dated, but in chnage log it shows creation date is actual creation date.

But in the following example showing Posting date and creation date is same (31-05-2024)

 

Regards,
Shubham Bulley

entry.title: Re: Enhanced Error Handling within SAP Digital Manufacturing Production Processes
entry.content:
Hello Manoel,

very interesting article that provides a quick introduction to error handling.

Whats a little confusing to me is the double usage of the word "errorcode".

For example the production process generates a Error with the error code "400" - and in the Business Service there is the error code "HTTP400" or "consumptionFailed" or what ever (not similar to the error code of the catch...)

... which is in my understanding more a message code then a error code ...

entry.title: Drop Down not working in Mobile App - SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are creating a Build app and we have 2 drop downs in it. We are binding it to the backend(SAP ECC) OData entity by using the MAP() function.

We have created a data variable and the data variable is set during the page mount event. We have removed the delay as well while getting the Data Collection. Data Collection is small enough where we don't need any pagination.

Though the dropdown works in the web preview but does not work with mobile preview. When we select a value from drop down it scrolls back to the TOP.

We tried adding another drop down from the Northwind service to try and that works fine. 

Any pointers will help.

 

Regards

Braham

entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!

entry.title: In Subscription Billing, what is diff between Custom Reference and Field catalogue?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

In Subscription Billing, I want to create a new field which will be received from external source in usage data or subscription. Could you please help me to understand if I shall define such fields in Custom References(Under Business configuration tile) or under Manage Field catalogue tile which is part of price calculation?

There are 3 use cases which I want to implement -

1. Accepting the fields in Usage and use them in price calculation 

2. Accepting the fields in Usage and use them in billing events as informative field(no logics on the field, only pass through)

3. Accepting fields in Subscription through contract creation

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: CJI3 submit program issue
entry.content:
You could either 

  • Check data existence with a call of FM LDB_PROCESS (simulating report call)
  • Debug the called report/transaction to find where to disable the message sending and return to caller

entry.title: Re: Comp mime type 'text/plain;charset=utf-8' instead ''
entry.content: It's a standard program, you should not fix it. It works well in my system. Just contact the SAP Support.

entry.title: Re: CDS Table function example to determine number of records
entry.content:
I believe you are talking about keeping results in temporary table/variable and use them further to make a final result set for the table function.

Yes. Thats my use case.

Let's say I want to read data from different database tables, e.g. info types and restructure this data to a final result of my table function. Is this possible?

In ABAP I would implement it like this:

TYPES BEGIN OF record_result.
TYPES record_dbtable_1 TYPE db_tab_1.
TYPES record_dbtable_2 TYPE db_tab_2.
TYPES END OF record_result.

TYPES records_result TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF record_result WITH EMPTY KEY.

SELECT
  FIELDS *
  FROM db_tab_1
  INTO TABLE DATA(db_tab_1).

SELECT
  FIELDS *
  FROM db_tab_2
  INTO TABLE DATA(db_tab_2).

DATA result TYPE records_result.

DATA record TYPE record_result.

record-dbtable_1 = db_tab_1.
record-dbtable_2 = db_tab_2.

INSERT record INTO TABLE result.

How could I do this using a table function?
I want to do the data reading and restructuring in the table function.

BR

entry.title: Re: False responsibilities in Signavio Process Manager RA...
entry.content:
Hi @istvan_palasthy 

It's often that incorrect mapping in the Signavio Process Manager leads to inaccuracies in the RACI Matrix. Double-check the process flow to ensure all responsibilities are assigned accurately.

If this doesn't resolve your issue, I would suggest you to reach out to SAP Support via SAP for me, as they can guide you through potential technical issues. Hope this helps!

Please do let me know again via this thread when you find a solution. It could also certainly help others facing a similar issue in future. 

Best,
Shreenith

entry.title: Re: Hybris 2211 : SmartEdit date picker prefilled with th...
entry.content:
No. you cannot do that in OOTB SmartEdit.

Could you please explain the use case of leaving start date/time empty? Thanks

entry.content:
I am trying to do Bank Reconciliation using SAP standard tempate in FIORI app Manage Bank Statement. In case where i have multiple receipts with same amount but different value in "Reference" and "Assignment" fields in the original receipt entry and i have updated the field "Item Refernce" with the same value. But system is not posting these lines and they need to be posted through reprocess option and then choosing the line to clear.

How is this scenario to be handled?

Thanks in advance for your help

entry.title: Re: Req to create 2 sales order using bapi with the data...

entry.title: Access all selected personnel numbers using LDB in HCM/HR
entry.content:
Hi there,

I am searching for a possibility to access all selected personnel numbers when using logical database in ABAP HCM/HR.

Example:
NODES: peras.
TABLES: pernr.

START-OF-SELECTION.

GET peras.

In the GET peras event an implicit loop is performed over all selected personnel numbers.
I want to know in which internal table these personnel numbers are stored in LDB and if I can access them.

I do not want to do anything like this as workaround:

DATA personnel_numbers TYPE STANDARD TABLE of persno.
GET peras.
INSERT pernr-pernr INTO TABLE personnel_numbers.

I am interessted in the underlying storage of the LDB!

Can anyone help out?

BR

entry.title: Purchase Order Number submissions for Cloud Partners now available via SAP for Me!
entry.content:
How to submit Purchase Order information in SAP for Me

In SAP for Me (S-User required), select “Customer Success” from the Dashboard menu:

Under the Renewals tab you will find the Upcoming Cloud Renewals and Missing Purchase Order card. On the “Missing PO Numbers“ section you will find all your End Customers with orders scheduled for billing in the upcoming 30 days which are missing a Purchase Order number. To display further information click on “Manage PO Numbers”.

On the pop up you will find all the required information to identify your contract. You may select the contracts you would like to update and provide the new purchase order ID or advise that no purchase order is required.

Once the Purchase order information is provided and you have selected from the two validity date options, you can review your data and confirm.

After confirming the information, the contract will no longer appear on the card. Please allow a few minutes for the system to process the updates.

A second option to update the Purchase Order information can be found on the End customer Details View. Scroll to the Cloud contracts for renewals card and click on “Manage PO Numbers” at the top of card. You will see the same pop up described above to make the purchase order update.

After confirming the information, the contract will no longer appear on the card. Please allow a few minutes for the system to process the updates.

In case you would like to update your purchase order responsible contact, contact your Cash Collection Team directly to submit the request. To find the appropriate e-mail address, refer to the Attachment section of SAP Knowledge Base Article 3107749 - How to reach the SAP Cash Collection Team for billing questions.

Don’t miss watching our short video clip which provides you with a quick overview on how easy your PO submission can be!

entry.title: End to end Configuration for Project Interest calculation
entry.content:
Interest calculation in SAP investment projects involves the process of determining and applying interest to the capital invested in a project over time. This calculation is crucial for accurately assessing the costs and financial performance of long-term investment projects. Here's a detailed explanation of how interest calculation works in SAP Investment Projects:

 

Key configuration Steps

Define Interest Indicator

 In this IMG activity, you create the interest indicator and determine the interest calculation type. A distinction is drawn between two types of interest calculation:

  • Account balance interest calculation (S)

With this type of interest calculation, the line items are first balanced and then interest is paid on the balance.
On projects, internal orders, capital investment orders, plant maintenance orders, and service orders, only account balance interest calculation is used.

  • Item interest calculation (P)

With this type of interest calculation, interest is paid on each individual payment transaction.

Activities

Create the required interest indicators.

Further notes

Furthermore, the most important determinations for interest are stored for an interest indicator in general and time-dependent conditions. These include, among other things, rules according to which interest is calculated and interest rates.

Define general terms

In this IMG activity, you determine the general conditions for each interest indicator. For all accounts which are part of the automatic interest calculation, you must specify the calendar type.

At this point, you can also:

  • determine the minimum/maximum amount for which interest calculation should take place
  • set the characteristics controlling the interest calculation indicator

Activities

1. Specify the calendar type for each interest calculation indicator.

2. Determine the controlling characteristics for each interest calculation indicator according to your requirements.

Define Reference Interest Rates

Define your reference interest rates by entering a reference interest rate as a key and a meaningful name. All other fields are purely informative.

Activities

1. Define your reference interest rates in this configuration step.

2. In the Enter Interest Rates configuration step, specify the interest rates for the reference interest rates.

Maintain Values for Reference Interest Rates

In this activity, you specify date-specific interest rates for the reference interest rates.

Activities

1. Specify the interest rates for your reference interest rates.

Define Time-Dependent Terms

In this IMG activity, you store the interest rates or reference interest rates for the key fields: Interest Indicator, Currency and amount. You can also do this, depending on the amount.

You must store interest rates for credit and debit interest.

You must define amount-dependent interest rates or reference interest rates for each movement type and date. The reference interest rates or interest rates stored for each Date from and movement type (for example, credit/debit-interest account balance interest calculation) determine the reference interest rates valid from this point in time and their grading based on the amount.

You can also store an interest rate independently of a reference interest rate.

Note

All data stored before this point in time is considered to be no longer valid after this point.

Consequently, when you make a change to the individual rates, you must always maintain all the appropriate rates for this movement type, interest indicator, and currency in the Date from field.

  • To continue to obtain interest-relevant values after switching to the euro, change the currency in the table to EURO.

Ensure that the validity date remains at the date before the changeover to the euro. You must do this to be able to completely transfer the entries from the time before the changeover.

Activities

  1. Per interest calculation indicator, currency, and date from, define:
    • The movement type identification code and
    • A reference interest rate (for example, discount rate) and/or
    • A surcharge

To store an interest rate independently of a reference interest rate, do not make an entry in the Reference interest rate field and enter a positive value in the Surcharge field.

  1. If necessary, also enter amount-dependent reference interest rates or interest rates per movement type and Date from.

Further notes

In the implementation guide for Accounting, under Business Volume > Interest Calculation, define the

  • Movement type
  • Reference interest rate

Create interest profile

In this IMG activity, you define the key and description of an interest profile and enter the basic settings for the interest calculation logic.

In the detail screen, you stipulate the following:

  • Hierarchy processing

Derive automatically

hierarchy process for the interest calculation is dependent on the project type. The interest logic and profile are inherited top-down within the hierarchy. The system distinguishes between the following project types:

    • Capital investment project

Balances are formed and the interest calculation updated for each WBS element for which a capital investment profile has been maintained. You must first settle assigned orders and networks to the WBS element if you want them to be included in the interest calculation.

    • Customer project

Balances are formed and the interest calculation updated in the billing element, taking account of all the lower-level objects in the hierarchy.

    • Cost project

Balances are formed and the interest calculation updated for each account assignment object.

Calculate interest separately

This is a manual indicator which overrides the automatic derivation in hierarchy processing. The system calculates interest for the object. Lower-level items without their own interest profile are not included. Lower-level items do not inherit the interest profiles of higher-level objects.

No interest calculation

This is a manual indicator which overrides the automatic derivation in hierarchy processing. The system does not calculate interest for the object and no attention is paid to lower-level items.

The manual interest calculation settings override the interest calculation derived automatically.

  • Document selection
    • Line items

The interest calculation is based on line cost, revenue, and payment line items as of the posting date.

    • Totals records

The interest calculation is based on the cost, revenue, and payment total records. Basic values are dated, for interest calculation purposes, as of the middle of the period.

Activities

  1. Define keys and a description for an interest profile.
  2. Maintain the detail screen for the interest profile.

Further notes

The system only calculates interest for a project or an internal order if you have defined an interest profile.

You do this in the control data as follows:

  • Standard project definition

In the menu for basic data, choose Standard structures -> Standard WBS -> Change -> Detail -> Control.

  • Project definition

The system defaults the entry to the WBS elements.

  • WBS elements

In the operative structures menu, choose Work breakdown struc -> Project definition or Structure (for WBS elements) -> Change -> Detail -> Control.

For internal orders, you can store the interest profile in:

  • Model order

To do this, choose Maintain Interest Profile in Model Orders.

  • In the master data for the relevant internal order

To do this, go to the internal orders menu and choose Master data -> Order -> Change.

Enter Detail Settings for Interest Profile

 

In the interest profile, you can store the following parameters for each interest indicator and currency:

  • Conditions for the project interest calculation:
    • Time elapsed since the first posting (minimum duration) and from which interest is calculated for the project or internal order
    • Threshold value (of the costs accumulated on a project or internal order) as of which interest is calculated on the project/internal order
  • Period control for the compound interest calculation
  •  Depreciation area for the interest base

Activities

Store the rules for the interest calculation.

Further notes

You have the following options for assigning an interest profile to a project:

  • As a default value in the Project System Implementation Guide, for:
    • The standard project definition
    • The project's definition
  • In the Project System application menu

If no interest profile is stored in the project profile, you can store an interest profile in the control data for:

    • Standard project definition

In the Basic Data menu, choose Standard structures -> Standard WBS -> Change -> Project definition -> Details -> Control.

    • Project definition

In the Operative Structures menu, choose Work breakdown struc -> Change -> Details -> Control.

The entry is copied into the WBS elements as a default value.

    • WBS elements

In the Operative Structures menu, choose Work breakdown structure -> Single element -> Change -> Details -> Control.

You can assign an interest profile to an internal order in one of the following ways:

  • In the model order:

To do this, choose Maintain Interest Profile in Model Orders.

  • In the master data of the relevant internal order:

To do this, go to the Internal Orders menu and choose Master Data -> Order -> Change.

Further notes

The planned interest calculation takes no account of the minimum duration and threshold value specifications.

Define Interest Relevance

In this IMG activity, you define value category relevance for an interest profile/interest indicator combination. You can do this for:

  • Costs/revenues
  • Payment data (down payments, payments) (not in internal orders)
  • No interest calculation

The value category consists of a range of cost elements and/or a range of commitment items..

Note the following:

Cost elements/commitment items not assigned to value categories are not included in the interest calculation.

For the purpose of the interest calculation, you must create interest in the form of cost elements. You can thus use the "None" indicator to control whether compound interest is calculated in an interest run.

Example

You want to calculate interest on payments and internal activities.

  • You want to calculate interest on the value categories for internal activities.

Select the cost indicator for the value category.

  • For external activity value categories, you want to calculate interest for the payment data (down payments and payments on the debit side and on the credit side) but not for the associated costs.

Select the Payments indicator for this value category.

Activities

Stipulate the relevance of the relevance of the interest..

Further notes

To avoid data problems, stipulate the interest relevance for every interest profile/interest indicator combination. If you do not maintain the interest indicator field, there is a wild card effect.

Specify Default Interest Profiles for Project Definitions

 

In this step, you assign the interest profile to the project profile.

Prerequisites

You must have maintained a project profile and an interest profile.

Further notes

The interest profile is copied into the project definition control data as a default value when you create a new project.

Define Posting Specifications

 

In this step, you define the posting profiles required for the interest calculation. In the posting profile, you configure account determination for each business transaction you need for the interest calculation.

This step is necessary if interest is to be recorded correctly in Controlling (CO).

Further notes

For technical reasons, the interest must first be recorded in the profit and loss accounts in FI. As the posting profile leads to both a debit posting and a credit posting, the resulting interest balance in FI is zero.

  • Change the currency in the table to the euro so that you continue to obtain interest-relevant values after switching to the euro.

This changeover is not necessary if you have already chosen a currency-independent setting by entering * in the relevant field.

Activities

You need two business transactions for the interest calculation: the interest-earned posting and interest-paid posting. You can maintain accounting determination so that it differs in the following characteristics for each business transaction:

  • Company code
  • Business area
  • Interest indicator

Maintain at least one posting profile for the interest-earned posting (business transaction 1000) and at least one for the interest-paid posting (business transaction 2000). Proceed as follows:

  1. Choose "Edit -> Create" and create the posting profiles you require.

If you do not want the account determinations to vary in respect of the characteristics mentioned above, enter the masking character "+".

Masking Example

Charact.

General Account detmn

Specific account detail

Business transaction

   1000

   1000

Company code

   0001

   0001

Interest indicator

     +

     01

Business area

     +

     +

  1. Choose Symbols to create the account symbols for business transactions of the same type.

The account symbols are used to group similar business transactions (such as interest-earned postings). The account symbols assign the transactions to a G/L account. This makes account determination flexible in cases where the postings to revenue and expenditure accounts are different.

  1. Choose Accounts.
  2. In the dialogue screen, enter the chart of accounts to which the account determination is to apply, then choose "Continue.".

This brings up the screen "Maintain Account Determination: Accounts.".

  1. Assign a G/L account to each account symbol.

You can make this assignment currency-dependent.

  1. Choose "Profile" to proceed to the "Posting Profile" view. Assign the account symbols to the debit and credit accounts.
  2. Use the "Goto" menu to choose the document type in which the interest posting is made.
  3. Check the account determination by choosing Goto > Simulation.

Further notes

Account determinations differentiated by company code, business area, or interest indicator take precedence over account determinations which are not differentiated.

You must not define any cost elements for the G/L accounts to which interest earned is posted. If you do, the system balances the interest postings off to zero.

If you are using Project Cash Management, a commitment item with a financial transaction other than 30 must be defined for the G/L accounts specified in the account symbol; otherwise, interest would be recorded in Project Cash Management.

Examples

You have maintained the following posting profile:

Business transaction: 2000

    Interest indicator:   +

Company code:      +

       Business area:  +

  

Debit posting

       1 Interest paid

Credit posting

       2 Correction: interest paid

  

The following G/L accounts are assigned to the account symbol:

Account symbol

G/L account

   1

483000

   2

222000

  

The system records the interest paid under G/L account 483000 and the correction under G/L account 222000. This ensures that the system shows no interest paid in FI.

For the interest paid to be recorded in CO, you must maintain cost element 483000.

You must not maintain a cost element for G/L account 222000. Otherwise, the interest-earned balance in CO is also zeroed out.

 

 

 

All the documentation I have used here is taken from SAP Nodes, so you should also check there for details.

This is the same configuration I have used for the process mapping of interest calculations in investment projects.

I would like to give special thanks to Subhalaxmi Barik for her support during the process mapping.

In my next blog, I will post some test results for the same configuration.

Thanks 

Dushyant Singh

entry.title: Set-up Alert Management in SAP Transportation Management.
entry.content:
Set-up Alert Management in SAP Transportation Management.

The alert management is a tool from SAP (Alert Monitor | SAP Help Portal) which provides numerous functions for handling alerts, such as representation rules, escalation, or notification using external communication methods.

Can be use e.g. in TM, EWM, Queue Monitor.

In this blog will explain how in SAP TM you can use the standard tool to send alert from some par of the process by email.

In alert management the Determines Recipients, can be used in 3 different possibilities.

  • Fixed Recipients
  • Subscription Authorization
  • Recipients Via User Roles

 Open the transaction ALRTCATDEF- Editing Alert Categories and go in the folder TM: Alert in Execution, in this part you will three standard types of alerts. I will use the TMS_TOR_GENERIC_ALERT.

1º Select the Alert category.

2ºDefinition of the properties.

3ºContainer (Technical part).

4ºText Information.

After you define the alert, you will use make sure in the Text Information is available, otherwise you will get the email but without any number of the Freight Document in the emails body. In case there is any text go the Container tab follow the steps bellow.

After these steps make sure your DEF_CHACO - Default Change Strategy, have these 2 steps add in:

  1. CHACOALPRP- Prepare Alerts for time, location, quantity change.
  2. CHACOALERT - Issue Alert for Change Controller

Even a Keyuser receipts an email, will be requested to open the Transaction ALRTINBOX - Alert Inbox which will open a page in the browser to see more details and take actions.

On the browser Key user can take an action, this means Key user can Not navigate from Alert management to any TM Object. Here key User will set as Complete, forward the alert. (Monitoring Alerts).

Refer to:

Alert Management (ALM) | SAP Help Portal

3037239 - Set-up Guide of Alert Management in Transportation Management - SAP for Me (Consider Attachments).

2971847 - Alert Management in SAP TM - SAP for Me

Hope this help you!

Thank you and regards.

Rogerio Lima.

entry.title: Dynamic Adjustments in SAP SuccessFactors Compensation: A Custom Solution
entry.content:
BUSINESS REQUIREMENT: 

Update the mandatory increase requirements in SAP SuccessFactors Compensation after launching the worksheets, without needing to delete and recreate them.

CUSTOM SOLUTION:

In order to update the mandatory increase requirements in SAP SuccessFactors Compensation without deleting and recreating the worksheets, a custom solution can be implemented. This custom approach allows for adjustments to be made directly within the system, ensuring that changes are applied efficiently without any disruptions or data loss. By leveraging this tailored solution, organizations can maintain compliance with new requirements while avoiding the time-consuming process of recreating compensation worksheets.

Step 1- Add a lookup Table:

To modify the default guideline for the Belgium population, set up a lookup table with the input as the country and an initial output value (n). This value can be updated later to enforce the default value.

Step 2- Add a new custom field and update the formula:

Create a new custom field and update the formula accordingly.

Step 3- Guideline Settings:

  • Navigate to the guideline you wish to set a default value for.
  • Select "Change Rule Settings."
  • Add the custom field (Mandatory Increase Custom Default) as one of the formula criteria.
  • Set "Force Default on Custom Field Change" to "Yes."
  • Save the changes. 

Loading the Guideline Formulae as per the requirement:

For the guideline criteria specifying the country "BEL" and a mandatory increase of "n," the default value will be set to 8, as illustrated in the next screenshot.  

When the customer needs to change the default value of a guideline for the live forms, update the lookup table and the default guideline as shown. Ensure all worksheets are updated to reflect this change.

CONCLUSION:

Implementing a custom solution to update mandatory increase requirements in SAP SuccessFactors Compensation offers a streamlined and efficient approach to managing compensation guidelines. By using a lookup table and custom fields, businesses can easily adjust default values without disrupting existing workflows or recreating worksheets. This method not only saves time but also ensures compliance and consistency across the compensation plan. Adopting such a flexible and dynamic solution enhances the overall effectiveness of compensation management in SAP SuccessFactors.

Hope this document helps!

Please feel free to share your thoughts and other solutions that you've experienced.

Thank you!
